title: Magnificently Crisp Portuguese Cream Skillet
description: A savory Portuguese dish featuring clotted cream and lacquer glaze, cooked to a crisp in an iron teppan, this satvik friendly recipe is a nostalgic treat for the senses, bringing together the richness of cream and the depth of lacquer glaze in perfect harmony.
instructions: Preheat the iron teppan over medium heat to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, whisk together the lacquer glaze, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the portuguese olive oil to the preheated teppan and swirl it around to coat the bottom.
Add the sliced onion to the teppan and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it is lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic to the teppan and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Pour the whisked lacquer glaze mixture over the onion and garlic in the teppan.
Add the chilled clotted cream to the teppan and stir gently to combine with the lacquer glaze mixture.
Cook for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cream is heated through and the mixture is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the teppan from the heat and sprinkle the chopped parsley over the top.
Let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
